LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS HARRY TOM, YOUNG TOM, KENNETH TOM AND ANA TELLEZ'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 22, 2016 AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, TO GIVE THE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.7B. PLAINTIFF MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JANUARY 12, 2016, AT 4:00 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS HARRY TOM, YOUNG TOM, KENNETH TOM AND ANA TELLEZ'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. A REVIEW OF THE ARBITRATOR'S AWARD INDICATES THAT THE ARBITRATOR DID NOT CONCLUDE THAT THE ENTIRE CONTRACT WAS ILLEGAL. EVEN THOUGH THE ARBITRATOR STATED THAT THE PETITIONERS WERE ENTITLED TO RESCISSION OF THE REAL ESTATE TRANSACTION INCLUDING THE PURCHASE AGREEMENT, THE ARBITRATOR'S ANALYSIS INDICATES THAT HE ONLY INTENDED FOR THE PORTION OF THE PURCHASE AGREEMENT WHICH ALLOWED FOR THE TRANSACTION TO OCCUR TO BE RESCINDED. FOR EXAMPLE, AFTER NOTING THAT THE PETITIONERS WERE ENTITLED TO RESCISSION OF THE PURCHASE AGREEMENT AND A RETURN OF THE $15,930 SECURITY DEPOSIT, THE ARBITRATOR STATED THAT PURSUANT TO "THE PURCHASE AGREEMENT, PARA. 21, AND CIVIL CODE SECTION 1057.3(B)(3) TOMS ARE ENTITLED TO REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES." (SEE PETITIONERS' ATTACHMENT 8 (C) PAGE 16.) THUS, CONTRARY TO RESPONDENTS' POSITION, THE ARBITRATOR DID NOT FIND THAT THE ENTIRE CONTRACT WAS ILLEGAL. FURTHERMORE, WHETHER A PARTICULAR PROVISION OF THE UNDERLYING CONTRACT IS ILLEGAL IS AN ARBITRABLE ISSUE AND HERE, THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT THE ARBITRATOR EXCEEDED HIS POWER BY FINDING THAT THE PORTION OF THE CONTRACT WHICH ALLOWED FOR THE TRANSFER OF PROPERTY TO OCCUR WAS ILLEGAL. (WOLITARSKY V. BLUE CROSS OF CALIF. (1997) 53 CA4TH 338, 344; CCP SECTION 1286.2.) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PETITIONER TOM, HARRY TOM, YOUNG HEE TOM, KENNETH TELLEZ, ANA RECOVER FROM RESPONDENT SUN, LEI SHEN, WENGIN $82,586.72 PRINCIPAL, TOTAL JUDGMENT $82,586.72, SEE SCANNED DOC

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Jun-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No apperances, tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

PETITIONERS HARRY TOM, YOUNG TOM, KENNETH TOM and ANA TELLEZ'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS is denied without prejudice to being refiled after the appeal of the April 14, 2016 order awarding them attorney's fees is resolved. Respondents' appeal of the April 14, 2016 order deprives this court of jurisdiction to award additional attorney's fees per CCP 916. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Apr-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Robert Capron telephone number 415-781-3355 appeared by telephone for Petitioner Harry Tom.
Tiffany Garrick of The Law Office of Kenny K. Tan telephone number 909-860-9008 appeared by telephone for Defendant Lei Sun.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. In re: PETITIONERS HARRY TOM, YOUNG TOM, KENNETH TOM and ANA TELLEZ's Motion For Award Of Attorney Fees And Costs - Petitioners are awarded fees of $15,000 and costs of $1516.80 for a total of $16,516.80. Only a small portion of the fees and $98 in costs are barred because the judgment has been satisfied. The court's review of the motion papers and the court file shows that $15,000 is a reasonable amount for the services covered by the fees request. Some of the services were in the nature of clerical, secretarial or paralegal work which should either be absorbed by overhead or should not be compensated at an attorney's hourly rate. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Petitioners Harry Tom, Young Tom, Kenneth Tom And Ana Tellez'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jan-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Capron of Law Office of Robert E. Capron telephone number 415-781-3355 for Petitioner.
Tiffany Garrick of The Law Office of Kenny K. Tan telephone number 909-860-9008 appeared by telephone for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioners Harry Tom, Young Tom, Kenneth Tom and Ana Tellez's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is granted. A review of the arbitrator's award indicates that the arbitrator did not conclude that the entire contract was illegal. Even though the arbitrator stated that the Petitioners were entitled to rescission of the real estate transaction including the Purchase Agreement, the arbitrator's analysis indicates that he only intended for the portion of the purchase agreement which allowed for the transaction to occur to be rescinded. For example, after noting that the Petitioners were entitled to rescission of the purchase agreement and a return of the $15,930 security deposit, the arbitrator stated that pursuant to "the Purchase Agreement, Para. 21, and Civil Code section 1057.3(b)(3) Toms are entitled to reasonable attorney's fees." (See Petitioners' Attachment 8 (c) page 16.) Thus, contrary to Respondents' position, the arbitrator did not find that the entire contract was illegal. Furthermore, whether a particular provision of the underlying contract is illegal is an arbitrable issue and here, there is no evidence that the arbitrator exceeded his power by finding that the portion of the contract which allowed for the transfer of property to occur was illegal. (Wolitarsky v. Blue Cross of Calif. (1997) 53 CA4th 338, 344; CCP section 1286.2.) The prevailing party is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Notice Of Hearing Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

PETITIONERS HARRY TOM, YOUNG TOM, KENNETH TOM AND ANA TELLEZ'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is continued to January 22, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, to give the Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.7B. Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 12, 2016, at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)
